passageNotes: 9.23.34 Ría de Pontevedra overview. Charts AC 3633, 1732/3; SC 416A/B; SHOM 3007. Standard Port LISBOA, ML 1·9. Tides: MHWS 3·8, MHWN 3·0, MLWN 1·5, MLWS 0·6; Differences MARIN (+0050 +0110 +0120 +0130, -0·4, -0·3, -0·1, 0·0). SHELTER: Good shelter from any wind; Islas Ons and Onza across mouth offer barrier to wind and swell. NAVIGATION: From WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W (also lies on 129° ldg lts for Ria de Vigo) track 053° to enter via main chan, Boca del Sudoeste, into centre of ria between Cabo de Udra and Pta Cabicastro. From NW small craft normally transit Canal de la Figilda on 125° or Canal de Los Camoucos on 180°; both approx 3ca wide, viable in rougher weather with due care. LIGHTS AND MARKS: Isla Ons steep cliffy rocky island with conspicuous octagonal lt ho. COMMUNICATIONS: Code 986; MRCC (981) 767320; Emergency 112; Marin VHF Ch 12, 16; Marinas VHF Ch 09.
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra

name: Porto Novo
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra
approachNote: WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W, track 053° to enter via main chan Boca del Sudoeste into centre of ria between Cabo de Udra and Pta Cabicastro
shelter: Good shelter from any wind; Islas Ons and Onza across mouth offer barrier to wind and swell

marina.name: Porto Novo (FV harbour with marina, 100+ berths) and Real Club Nautico
marina.notes: FV harbour with marina (100+ berths) and Real Club Nautico. Ferry to Isla Ons.

name: Nauta Sanxenxo
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra
approachNote: WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W, track 053° into ria. SHM lt bcn marks drying rocks on N side entrance. Avoid busy ferry pier.
shelter: Good shelter from any wind; Islas Ons and Onza across mouth offer barrier to wind and swell

marina.name: Nauta Sanxenxo
marina.notes: Max LOA 45m. Wi-fi, max 80t (5t). SHM lt bcn marks drying rocks on N side entrance. Avoid busy ferry pier.

name: Real Club de Sanxenxo
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra
approachNote: WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W, track 053° into ria. Pontoon just N of marina, close to beach.
shelter: Good shelter from any wind; Islas Ons and Onza across mouth offer barrier to wind and swell

marina.name: Real Club de Sanxenxo
marina.notes: €3-05 inc. max LOA 12m. Pontoon just N of marina, close to beach.

name: Raxó
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra
approachNote: WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W, track 053° into ria. Temp'y anchorage in about 5m off bkwtr hd.

name: Combarro
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra
approachNote: WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W, track 053° into ria. AC 1733. 287 + 45 <12m and 2 max 20m LOA, €2.80/m. Good depths in marina and channel but do not pass right of Isla Tambo; leave mussel rafts to port in final approach.

marina.name: Combarro marina
marina.notes: 287 + 45 berths (<12m) and 2 berths max 20m LOA. Max 50t (6t).

name: Pontevedra
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra
approachNote: WPT 42°18'·00N 08°57'·16W, track 053° into ria. Mouth of Rio Lérez. LW dinghy recce of shallow chan between training walls advised; least depth 1·3m. Access restricted by overhead cables (19m clearance) and motorway bridge (12m clearance).
shelter: Good shelter; provincial capital with all domestic facilities

passageNotes: Ría de Pontevedra continued (p.994). Marin 42°23'·94N 08°42'·21W: naval, commercial and FV port; no yacht facilities (tiny marina in W basin, private). Isla Tambo NW is restricted military area; landing prohibited. Aguete 42°22'·55N 08°44'·16W: Marina, approx 100 berths, little space for yachts but many anchored outside; to clear offlying rocks round SHM buoy before turning S and E/NE of marinas, clear of fairway to FV jetty. Real Club de Mar, Village few shops. Bueu 42°19'·79N 08°47'·02W: FV harbour, no alongside berthing, anchor to W of hbr in about 5m. Mussel rafts to stbd as chartlet. HM 320042. Good shops, ferry to Isla Ons. Beluso 42°20'·01N 08°47'·90W: Mussel beds (viveiros) E/NE of hbr; 2nd pontoon to stbd is for yachts, F&A berths, but access to shore may be difficult due to coded gates. HM 400870. Excellent anchorage, 20 min walk to Bueu. Ria de Aldán: Punta del Con at 42°20'·01N 08°47'·90W, head of Ria; worth exploring in settled weather; beware mussel rafts on W side. Isla Ons: almost uninhabited, very popular with tourists in season; Almacén anchorage 42°23'·33N 08°52'·57W in 5-8m clear of extensive moorings; fair weather anchorage in 5m off Melide beach 42°23'·37N 08°52'·50W but within National Park; no landing on Isla Onza (bird sanctuary).
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Pontevedra (continued)

passageNotes: 9.23.35 Ría de Vigo overview. Pontevedra 42°13'·85N 08°48'·26W (centre of precautionary area). Charts AC 3633, 1730, 1731; SC 416, 416B; SHOM 7595. Standard Port LISBOA, ML 1·96. Tides: MHWS 3·8, MHWN 3·0, MLWN 1·5, MLWS 0·6; Differences VIGO (+0040 +0100 +0105 +0125, 0·0, -0·3, 0·0, -0·2). SHELTER: Ria sheltered by Islas Cies, attractive anchorages on E side; sheltered anchorages in Ens de San Simon; 3 marinas near Vigo centre well sheltered unless NW'ly swell enters. NAVIGATION: N Chan (129° ldg lts) WPT 42°16'·09N 08°53'·94W, thence 160°/2M via northern TSS to intersect 095° ldg line. S Chan (065° ldg lts) WPT 42°09'·42N 08°54'·86W, thence via southern TSS to intersect 041° ldg line. Precautionary area centred on intersection of 095° and 041° ldg lines; small craft should not impede big ships in TSS and/or Precautionary area. LIGHTS AND MARKS: Islas Cies identified by high rugged bare slopes and white beaches on E side; ldg lts/marks for approach chans as per chartlet and Lights, buoys and waypoints. COMMUNICATIONS: Code 986; MRCC 222230; Emergency 112; Port Control (902)109356; Brit Consul (902)109356; Marinas VHF Ch 09; Club Náutico de Rodiera (Cangas) Ch 06; 269414; 268200; Turismo 224757.
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo

name: Cangas
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: N Chan WPT 42°16'·09N 08°53'·94W, thence 160°/2M via northern TSS to intersect 095° ldg line. Cangas small friendly YC marina in NW corner of Ensenada de Cangas. Approx €2.50; 64t (8t). If full anchor SE of marina, clear of ferries and FVs.

marina.name: Club Náutico de Rodiera / Cangas marina
marina.notes: Approx €2.50; 64t (8t). YC with English spoken. Ferry to Vigo ½ hourly.

name: Moaña Marina
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: N Chan WPT 42°16'·09N 08°53'·94W. Approach from S, passing El Con hbr. Avoid viveros to NE and E. Fingers too short – telephone before arrival.

marina.name: Moaña Marina
marina.notes: Limited berths on fingers at N end of 1st pontoon (other fingers too short). €1.37.

name: Domayo Marina
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: N Chan WPT 42°16'·09N 08°53'·94W. 7ca W of suspension bridge between Domayo hbr Fl R 5s and conspicuous factory quay to bridge. Best approach from SW, close inshore of fish farms (viveros). NE.

name: Ensenada de San Simón
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: AC 1730. Peaceful anchorages in 3-4m mud off W, E and S sides, clear of viveros. €2.50; anchoring only (pontoon).
shelter: Peaceful anchorage in 3-4m mud off W, E and S sides, clear of viveros

name: San Adrián de Cobres Marina
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: Only the It, close NW of the marina. 204 berths on fingers inc; max LOA 18m, €1.25. R 11s is the only lt, close NW of marina. Approach from NE.

marina.name: Marina San Adrián de Cobres
marina.notes: Max LOA 18m. 204 berths on fingers inc.

name: Marina Dávila Sport
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: S Chan WPT 42°09'·42N 08°54'·86W, thence via southern TSS to intersect 041° ldg line. Darsena de Bouzas, staff speak good English and French.

marina.name: Marina Dávila Sport
marina.notes: Modern repair facilities for all yacht sizes, highly recommended. Many amenities. CN 232442.

name: Vigo Marina
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: N Chan WPT 42°16'·09N 08°53'·94W. Narrow ent between PHM/SHM lt twrs. VHF Ch 09 (both H24). Between PHM/SHM lt twrs.

marina.name: Vigo Marina (Puerto Deportivo)
marina.notes: F&A berths, in west basin (Darsena de A Laxe) max 10m LOA; east basin (Darsena de AS Avenidas) >10m to max 35m LOA. Wi-fi security H24, key card access. 32t (3t). RCN clubhouse 433588.

name: Pta Lagoa Marina
region: N & NW Spain – Ría de Vigo
approachNote: N Chan WPT 42°16'·09N 08°53'·94W. 2½km N of city centre.

marina.name: Pta Lagoa Marina / Yatesport
marina.notes: VHF 09. 250 + 100 berths. Max LOA 24m. Draft 3·5m. 2½km N of city.

name: Bayona
approachNote: WPT 42°07'·78N 08°55'·06W (see Ria de Vigo chartlet) 084°/2·8M to SPM buoy QY. From S keep 1M off Cabo Silleiro to clear reefs marked by NCM buoy. Las Serralleiras, rocks/islets N of ldg line are marked by SCM & WCM lt buoys and by a lt bcn. Canal de la Porta between Monte Ferro and Las Estelas is a useful shortcut to/from Ria de Vigo, but hold close to island to avoid 0·9m patch and rock which dries close W of mid-channel.
shelter: Excellent in both marinas; wavebreak at Bayona Marina 20m NE of pontoons, now has low level Q.Fl.Y lts at each end

marina.name: Bayona Marina (Monte Real Club de Yates – MRCY + Bayona Marina)
marina.notes: Bayona Marina: 297 + 190 berths, €3.00/m, VHF Ch 09, mob 626299162. MRCY: 200 F&A + some anchorages in 6m, €2.95, multihulls +50%; max 20t (1·5t); Clubhouse very well appointed. Secretaria@mrcyb.com www.mrcyb.es VHF Ch 71, 385000. Dique de Abrigo (white wall) has G/W tower QG. Ldg/Dir lts 084° as chartlet. Town: most usual requirements inc buses to Vigo (21km).

passageNotes: 9.24.2 Portugal – Viana do Castelo to Vila Real de Santo António, and The Azores. Area overview page with passage chart covering the full Portuguese coast. Magnetic variation 1·8°W (N) to 1·0°W (S). Off Cape Roca TSS: ITZ bounded by Lat 38°52'N to N; brg/dist 253°/9M from Cabo Raso to S; extends approx 9M west of Cape Roca to 09°41'W. Lights of Cabo Roca: Fl(4) 17s and Fl(3) 9s. Yachts should monitor Ch 16 whilst in the ITZ. TSS extends further 20M west to 10°13'·75W; outer N and S-bound lanes used by vessels carrying dangerous cargoes in bulk. Off Cape St Vincent TSS: ITZ bounded by Lat 37°01'N (Cape St Vincent, Fl 5s) to N; brg/dist 186°/9M from Punta de Sagres; extends in segmented arc approx 9M west and south; yachts should monitor Ch 16 whilst in ITZ. COPREP participation mandatory for large vessels; yachts that elect to participate call Roca Control (CSC229) H24 at reporting points; MMSI 002633030; VHF Ch 16, 22, 69, 79. Harbours listed: 9.24.6 Viana do Castelo, 9.24.7 Póvoa de Varzim, 9.24.8 Leixões, 9.24.9 Porto, 9.24.10 Aveiro, 9.24.11 Figueira da Foz, 9.24.12 Nazaré, 9.24.13 Peniche, 9.24.14 Cascais, 9.24.15 Lisboa, 9.24.16 Sesimbra, 9.24.17 Setúbal, 9.24.18 Sines, 9.24.19 Lagos, 9.24.20 Portimão, 9.24.21 Albufeira, 9.24.22 Faro/Olhão, 9.24.23 Tavira, 9.24.24 Vila Real de Santo António. Distance table included (Longships to Gibraltar, 20 places).
region: Portugal – Viana do Castelo to Vila Real de Santo António
